title: Budapest pizzeria offers time-travel twist with ancient Rome-inspired pie
description: BUDAPEST, Hungary — In Hungary’s capital, a city best known for its goulash, a pizzeria is inviting diners to travel back two millennia to a time before tomatoes, mozzarella or even the word “pizza” were known in Europe.

title: Why ancient Chinese folk custom involved cutting thigh flesh to feed sick relatives
description: In a bizarre and gruesome blend of medical belief and extreme filial devotion, many people throughout Chinese history cut flesh from their own thighs to heal sick relatives.The custom later evolved into a government-endorsed trend, turning the practice into a path to social mobility and marital success.Ge gu liao qin, which literally means “cutting one’s own flesh to heal a parent”, refers to an ancient practice in which a child would cut flesh from their own thigh to use as medicine to treat...

title: Gas price shocks from Middle East crisis proves Australia must unhook itself from volatile fossil fuels
description: SYDNEY, Wednesday 4 March 2026 — As the Middle East crisis sends global gas and oil prices surging, Greenpeace Australia Pacific warns that peace and security will remain at the mercy of geopolitics as long as we remain hooked on fossil fuels. Experts have warned the outbreak of war in the Middle East, and the ... Continued

title: Warning after ancient pit exposed in Aussie gold rush town
description: An ancient mine site has been unearthed by vandals in a historic Aussie Gold Rush town, and now authorities are urging anyone with any information to come forward. Pictures supplied to Yahoo News show that the metal cap to a deep shaft in Victoria’s Growlers Hill mine had been removed, with the state’s environment department warning that the offender had left “a dangerous situation where someone could be seriously injured or killed.” The department added in a statement, “This is not harmless fun, it’s dangerous.” “This reckless behaviour puts lives at risk and ruins shared spaces.” The historic site operated as a mine between 1854 and 1918, and while there was ample gold, the underground water table ultimately forced the site to close.
